allowed_assumptions: Record values for key fields are hashable.
constraints: Must NOT use nested loops for comparison.
disallowed_assumptions: All records have all key fields.
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement `find_duplicates(records: list[dict], keys: list[str]) -> list[list[dict]]`.

Find groups of records that are duplicates based on a subset of fields (the `keys` parameter).
Two records are duplicates if all values for the specified keys are equal.
Return a list of groups, where each group contains the records that are duplicates of each other.
Records with unique key combinations are not returned.

Constraint: Must use a hash map (dict), not nested loops.
Complexity must be O(n) average, not O(n²).

Handle: keys list empty (treat as no grouping, return []), records missing one of the key fields (use None as default), None values in key fields (treat as a valid comparison value).
public_contract: find_duplicates(records, keys) -> list[list[dict]] with O(n) average complexity
title: Avoid O(n²) Duplicate Scanning
